primary_behavior: Input validation: the pack declares regulation_id REG-B, the requirement it contains declares REG-A. Validation must fail before any applicability or compliance analysis.

expected_outcome.must_request: a corrected, versioned requirement pack in which the requirement's regulation_id matches its containing pack
must_not_do: fabricate any status from the practice data
guess which regulation_id is the correct one
escalate to the CCO — this is a malformed input, not a compliance finding

notes: The practice record and runtime context are complete and would support a clean assessment — that is the trap. A well-formed-looking bundle with one corrupted linkage must halt at validation. Reporting a status of `unknown` is also a failure here: no classification of any kind should be produced.
